title: Comforting Charred British Beef Shepherd's Pie
description: A hearty, comforting British dish featuring charred beef, castelvetrano olives, and a crispy mashed potato topping. Perfect for a chilly evening in.

ingredients.name: 1.5 lbs beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Choose a cut with good marbling for the best flavor

ingredients.name: 3-4 medium-sized potatoes, peeled and roughly chopped

instructions: In a large Dutch oven or pot, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot, whisking constantly.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the peas and carrots and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Add the castelvetrano olives to the pot and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Mash the potatoes in a separate bowl until they're smooth, then add the butter and milk to the bowl and mash until the potatoes are creamy.
Spread the mashed potatoes over the top of the beef mixture in the baking dish, making sure to cover the entire surface.
Use a spatula or spoon to create a decorative border around the edge of the potatoes.
Bake the shepherd's pie in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
